title: Worker test that exposes atom-key bug in a perform/1 pattern match
prompt: Write a test `MyApp.Workers.BrokenWorkerTest` that demonstrates WHY `perform_job/3` is critical: it auto-converts args to string keys before calling perform/1, so a worker that incorrectly pattern-matches on atom keys will fail the test (not appear to pass). The test uses `Oban.Testing` and `perform_job(BrokenWorker, %{user_id: 1})` (note: atom key in the args passed in). Assert that the test fails with a `FunctionClauseError` if `BrokenWorker.perform/1` matches on `%{user_id: _}` (atoms), OR passes with `:ok` if `BrokenWorker.perform/1` matches on `%{"user_id" => _}` (strings). Include a short `@moduledoc` citing the Oban.Testing docs that the helper 'automatically converts args to string keys before calling perform/1, ensuring that perform clauses aren't erroneously trying to match atom keys.' Write to `test/my_app/workers/broken_worker_test.exs`.
tier_rationale: Meta-level test that uses Oban.Testing's own string-key conversion to prove a worker is broken. Requires understanding the helper's role as a guardrail.
research_citation: Oban.Testing HexDocs — 'it automatically converts args to string keys before calling perform/1, ensuring that perform clauses aren't erroneously trying to match atom keys.'
